response.justification: The candidate is primarily a web and full-stack developer whose core stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. C++ appears only briefly in one early test-and-measurement role at National Instruments and in a thesis project, with no indication of sustained Windows-platform C++ development approaching the mandatory 5–10 years. Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, Capital Markets, and Investment Banking domain knowledge are entirely absent from both employment history and self-described skills. SQL Server and Oracle are not referenced anywhere, and SQL exposure appears superficial. The candidate fails to satisfy the mandatory technical and domain requirements for this role.
strengths: Has direct C and C++ exposure writing components for NI LabWindows/CVI, confirming at least some compiled-systems grounding
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing track record via workshops, conference talks, and open-source community work
Some financial-services adjacency through a role focused on investment practices in Germany, providing a thin baseline of industry familiarity
concerns: C++ experience is limited, dated, and not tied to Windows platform — the mandatory 5–10 year requirement is clearly not met
No Fixed Income, Derivatives, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain knowledge present anywhere in the resume
SQL Server and Oracle are absent from skills and work history; SQL fluency appears minimal and undocumented

key_factors.factor: No capital markets or fixed income domain knowledge
CI/CD and Java background aligns with optional desirable skills
